analysis_methodology.upstream_analysis: Identified all programs that directly INCLUDE this copybook and traced their callers
analysis_methodology.downstream_analysis: Traced programs called by direct users to identify transitive impact
analysis_methodology.shared_data_analysis: Analyzed which programs share this copybook and other copybooks used by ACCOUNT-MGR
analysis_methodology.impact_scoring: Applied scoring matrix based on: direct callers (30%), transitive depth (20%), copybook breadth (25%), complexity (15%), risk flags (10%)

data_items.description: Account identifier (primary key); used in indexed file access
Customer ID foreign key; links to CUSTOMER-RECORD.CUST-ID; critical for data relationships
Account type with 88-level conditions (ACCT-CURRENT, ACCT-SAVINGS, ACCT-LOAN); changing length or values breaks EVALUATE logic
Account status with 88-level conditions (ACCT-ACTIVE, ACCT-INACTIVE, ACCT-CLOSED); controls business logic flow in ACCOUNT-MGR.3000-UPDATE-ACCOUNT
Monetary balance in packed decimal format; used in financial calculations; COMP-3 requires conversion for cloud; precision/scale changes could cause data loss
Credit/debit limit in packed decimal format; used in validation logic; COMP-3 requires conversion for cloud; precision/scale changes could cause data loss
Account opening date (YYYYMMDD); used for audit and reporting

cloud_portability_concerns.mitigation: COMP-3 (packed decimal) is mainframe-specific; requires conversion to standard numeric types (e.g., BigDecimal in Java) for cloud deployment

affected_programs.reason: Directly includes ACCOUNT-RECORD copybook; core account management logic; reads/writes ACCOUNT-FILE; uses condition names for business logic

copybook_usage.cross_copybook_coupling: HIGH - ACCOUNT-MGR uses all 3 copybooks together; business logic couples data from multiple sources

condition_name_usage: ACCT-TYPE conditions (ACCT-CURRENT, ACCT-SAVINGS, ACCT-LOAN) - any length/value changes break type checking
ACCT-STATUS conditions (ACCT-ACTIVE, ACCT-INACTIVE, ACCT-CLOSED) - used in EVALUATE statement in 3000-UPDATE-ACCOUNT
comp3_calculations: ACCT-BALANCE used in financial calculations and comparisons
ACCT-LIMIT used for credit limit validation

impact_score.adjusted_level: HIGH (elevated due to direct usage, COMP-3 fields, and cross-copybook coupling)

key_concerns: CRITICAL: Contains COMP-3 fields (ACCT-BALANCE, ACCT-LIMIT) - cloud migration requires numeric type conversion
HIGH: Uses 88-level condition names extensively - any field length/value changes break logic
HIGH: Cross-copybook coupling with CUSTOMER-RECORD and PAYMENT-RECORD
MEDIUM: Indexed file access via ACCT-CUST-ID - changes to this field affect file I/O

affected_programs.reason: Calls ACCOUNT-MGR which directly uses ACCOUNT-RECORD; changes to copybook structure may require updates to calling parameters

copybook_usage.cross_copybook_coupling: MEDIUM - Passes CUSTOMER-REC to ACCOUNT-MGR; impacts via parameter interface

key_concerns: Integration testing required with ACCOUNT-MGR to verify parameter passing
Regression testing for customer processing workflow
Called by BATCH-RUNNER - impacts batch processing

affected_programs.reason: Entry point program that calls CUSTOMER-PROC; two levels removed from ACCOUNT-RECORD usage

copybook_usage.cross_copybook_coupling: LOW - Minimal coupling; primarily batch orchestration

key_concerns: Full end-to-end batch regression testing required
Entry point program - impacts entire batch workflow

affected_programs.reason: Called by ACCOUNT-MGR; may receive account data via parameters; contains GOTO statements (HIGH risk)
affected_programs.usage_type: Downstream via CALL statement from ACCOUNT-MGR

copybook_usage.cross_copybook_coupling: MEDIUM - Receives payment data from ACCOUNT-MGR; may include ACCT-BALANCE mappings

impact_score.adjusted_level: MEDIUM (elevated due to GOTO statements and downstream coupling)

key_concerns: CRITICAL: Contains 2x GOTO statements (lines 50, 58) - HIGH complexity risk
MEDIUM: Receives payment data from ACCOUNT-MGR - may be impacted by ACCT-BALANCE changes

call_hierarchy.description: Complete call chain showing impact propagation

shared_data_dependencies.description: Other copybooks used by affected programs and data relationships

copybooks.risk: HIGH - ACCT-CUST-ID links to CUST-ID; referential integrity must be maintained
HIGH - ACCT-BALANCE mapped to PAY-AMOUNT; precision/scale changes propagate

shared_data_dependencies.cross_copybook_contamination: HIGH - ACCOUNT-MGR uses all 3 copybooks together (CUSTOMER-RECORD, ACCOUNT-RECORD, PAYMENT-RECORD); business logic couples data from multiple copybooks; changes to ACCOUNT-RECORD require coordinated impact analysis of other copybooks

field_relationships.impact: CRITICAL - Changes to ACCT-CUST-ID field affect customer-account relationship
HIGH - COMP-3 precision/scale changes may cause data truncation or financial errors
HIGH - Status synchronization logic in ACCOUNT-MGR.3000-UPDATE-ACCOUNT

file_io_impact_analysis.description: Files that may be impacted by ACCOUNT-RECORD changes

files.risk: CRITICAL - Changes to ACCOUNT-RECORD structure require corresponding changes to file layout; indexed file requires reorg if key field changes

recommended_change_sequence.description: Document all field mappings between ACCOUNT-RECORD, CUSTOMER-RECORD, and PAYMENT-RECORD; identify COMP-3 conversion requirements for cloud
deliverables: COMP-3 to Java BigDecimal conversion strategy

recommended_change_sequence.description: Backup ACCTMAST file; create isolated test environment with sample data
Update copybook structure; ensure backward compatibility where possible; document all changes
Modify ACCOUNT-MGR to handle copybook changes; update field mappings; adjust COMP-3 handling; recompile and unit test
recommended_change_sequence.validation: Unit tests for all paragraphs; condition name testing; file I/O validation

recommended_change_sequence.description: Verify parameter passing to ACCOUNT-MGR; update if needed; integration testing
recommended_change_sequence.validation: Integration tests between CUSTOMER-PROC and ACCOUNT-MGR

recommended_change_sequence.description: Test payment processing logic; verify data mappings from ACCOUNT-RECORD fields; address GOTO impacts
recommended_change_sequence.validation: Payment processing regression tests; financial calculation validation

recommended_change_sequence.description: Run BATCH-RUNNER with test data; end-to-end workflow validation
recommended_change_sequence.validation: Complete batch cycle execution; output verification

recommended_change_sequence.description: Update ACCOUNT-FILE (ACCTMAST) layout if needed; migrate data; deploy to production
recommended_change_sequence.validation: Production smoke tests; file integrity validation

risk_factors.description: ACCT-BALANCE and ACCT-LIMIT use COMP-3 format; mainframe-specific; requires conversion for cloud deployment; precision/scale changes could cause financial data loss
risk_factors.mitigation: Implement robust numeric conversion with precision validation; use Java BigDecimal for financial calculations; comprehensive testing of monetary operations

risk_factors.description: Extensive use of condition names for ACCT-TYPE and ACCT-STATUS; changing field lengths or values breaks business logic
risk_factors.mitigation: Maintain backward compatibility; use constants/enums in migration; comprehensive condition name testing

risk_factors.description: ACCOUNT-MGR uses ACCOUNT-RECORD, CUSTOMER-RECORD, and PAYMENT-RECORD together; tightly coupled business logic
risk_factors.mitigation: Coordinated change management across all three copybooks; synchronized testing; field mapping validation

risk_factors.description: ACCOUNT-FILE is indexed by ACCT-ID; changes to key field require file reorganization
risk_factors.mitigation: If key field changes, plan file reorganization; coordinate with DBA; off-peak deployment

risk_factors.description: PAYMENT-HANDLER contains GOTO statements; difficult to maintain and test; may be affected by parameter changes
risk_factors.mitigation: Refactor GOTO statements before major changes; comprehensive regression testing; consider structural redesign

risk_factors.description: Only ACCOUNT-MGR directly uses this copybook; limits blast radius
risk_factors.mitigation: Focus testing on ACCOUNT-MGR and its immediate callers/callees

mitigation_strategies: Phase 1: Refactor PAYMENT-HANDLER GOTO statements BEFORE modifying ACCOUNT-RECORD
Phase 2: Create comprehensive test suite for COMP-3 numeric conversions
Phase 3: Implement field mapping validation between all 3 copybooks
Phase 4: Coordinate with DBA for file layout changes and deployment timing
Phase 5: Implement rollback plan with file backups and version control

focus_areas: Condition name logic for ACCT-TYPE and ACCT-STATUS
Status synchronization between CUSTOMER-RECORD and ACCOUNT-RECORD

test_levels.scope: Complete batch workflow (BATCH-RUNNER -> CUSTOMER-PROC -> ACCOUNT-MGR -> PAYMENT-HANDLER)

test_data_requirements: Account records with all ACCT-TYPE values (CUR, SAV, LON)
Account records with all ACCT-STATUS values (A, I, C)
COMP-3 boundary value testing (max/min for ACCT-BALANCE and ACCT-LIMIT)
Edge cases: zero balances, negative balances, limit overruns

assumptions: No major structural changes to ACCOUNT-RECORD (e.g., no field removals)
No concurrent changes to CUSTOMER-RECORD or PAYMENT-RECORD
DBA availability for file reorganization if needed
risks_to_schedule: COMP-3 conversion issues may extend testing (+2 days)
Cross-copybook coordination may require additional analysis (+1 day)
PAYMENT-HANDLER GOTO refactoring may be required first (+3 days)
File reorganization may require extended downtime (+0.5 days)

immediate_actions: 1. Schedule architecture review to discuss COMP-3 conversion strategy
2. Document all field mappings between ACCOUNT-RECORD, CUSTOMER-RECORD, and PAYMENT-RECORD
3. Create isolated test environment with representative data
4. Consider refactoring PAYMENT-HANDLER GOTO statements BEFORE changing ACCOUNT-RECORD
code_quality_improvements: 1. Refactor PAYMENT-HANDLER to eliminate GOTO statements
2. Add inline documentation for all condition name usage
3. Implement validation routines for COMP-3 field conversions
4. Consider separating cross-copybook coupling into separate modules
long_term_strategy: 1. Plan for eventual cloud migration - prioritize COMP-3 elimination
2. Consider microservices architecture to reduce program coupling
3. Implement automated regression testing for financial calculations
4. Establish data governance for copybook changes across the enterprise

blockers.solution: Convert to Java BigDecimal or equivalent; implement custom serialization
Migrate to relational database (e.g., PostgreSQL) with proper indexing

migration_sequence: 1. Convert COMP-3 fields to standard numeric types
3. Migrate ACCOUNT-MGR business logic to Java service
4. Implement REST API for account management operations
5. Preserve 88-level conditions as enums or constants

notification_list.reason: ACCOUNT-FILE layout changes may require file reorganization
Comprehensive testing strategy requires 6.5 days of QA effort
Validate condition name logic and business rules
Integration testing and parameter interface validation
Downstream impact analysis and GOTO refactoring discussion

cypher_queries_used.description: Cypher queries that would be used with Neo4j MCP tools for this analysis

queries.purpose: Find all programs that include ACCOUNT-RECORD copybook

queries.query: MATCH path = (upstream:Program)-[:CALLS*1..10]->(target:Program {program_id: 'ACCOUNT-MGR'}) RETURN upstream.program_id, length(path) AS call_depth, upstream.estimated_complexity ORDER BY call_depth ASC

queries.purpose: Find downstream programs called by ACCOUNT-MGR
queries.query: MATCH path = (target:Program {program_id: 'ACCOUNT-MGR'})-[:CALLS*1..10]->(downstream:Program) RETURN downstream.program_id, length(path) AS depth, downstream.estimated_complexity ORDER BY depth ASC
MATCH (p:Program {program_id: 'ACCOUNT-MGR'})-[:INCLUDES]->(cb:Copybook) RETURN cb.name AS copybook ORDER BY cb.name

queries.purpose: Find all data items in ACCOUNT-RECORD
queries.query: MATCH (cb:Copybook {name: 'ACCOUNT-RECORD'})-[:DEFINES]->(di:DataItem) RETURN di.fqn, di.name, di.level, di.pic, di.usage ORDER BY di.fqn

report_generation_metadata.data_quality: Excellent - parsed from actual COBOL source files
limitations: Neo4j MCP tools not available - analysis based on static graph data
Manual correlation of cross-copybook field mappings from source code
COMP-3 conversion complexity estimates based on industry standards
